name: Golf Knowledge
description: Rules, majors, courses and legends from the world of golf.

easy.question: What is the term for finishing a hole one stroke under par?
easy.explanation: One under par is a birdie; two under is an eagle, one over is a bogey.

easy.question: What is the sand-filled hazard on a golf course called?
easy.explanation: Bunkers are sand hazards — escaping them cleanly is one of golf's trickiest skills.

easy.question: How many holes make up a full standard round of golf?
easy.explanation: A full round is 18 holes — a convention cemented at St Andrews in 1764.

easy.question: Which club is used on the green?
easy.explanation: The putter rolls the ball along the green and into the hole.

easy.question: In golf, the player with the LOWEST score wins.
easy.explanation: True — golf is scored by strokes taken, so fewer is better.

easy.question: Why do golfers shout "Fore!"?
options: To warn people a ball is heading their way
easy.explanation: "Fore!" warns anyone in the ball's path to take cover.

easy.question: Which club typically hits the ball the farthest?
easy.explanation: The driver has the longest shaft and lowest loft of the standard clubs, built for maximum distance off the tee.

easy.question: What is a score of two strokes under par on a hole called?
easy.explanation: Two under par is an eagle. Three under is an albatross — far rarer.

easy.question: What is the closely mown strip between the tee and the green called?
easy.explanation: The fairway is the short grass you're aiming for — the rough lines either side of it.

easy.question: What marks the position of the hole on the green?
easy.explanation: The flagstick, or pin, stands in the cup so the hole is visible from distance.

easy.question: Each hole begins from the teeing area.
easy.explanation: True — every hole starts with a tee shot from the teeing ground.

easy.question: What does "par" represent on a hole?
options: The expected number of strokes for an expert golfer
The average of all players' scores
easy.explanation: Par is the score an expert golfer is expected to make — it sets the benchmark for birdies and bogeys.

medium.question: Which famous course hosts The Masters every year?
medium.explanation: The Masters is the only major played at the same venue every year: Augusta National in Georgia, USA.

medium.question: What is the maximum number of clubs allowed in a player's bag?
medium.explanation: The Rules of Golf allow a maximum of 14 clubs.

medium.question: The Ryder Cup is contested between which two teams?
options: USA and the Rest of the World
medium.explanation: Since 1979 the Ryder Cup has pitted the USA against a combined Team Europe.

medium.question: What is a score of three strokes under par on a single hole called?
medium.explanation: Three under is an albatross (double eagle in the US). Four under — the condor — has only ever been recorded a handful of times.

medium.question: Which legendary golfer is nicknamed "The Golden Bear"?
medium.explanation: Jack Nicklaus, winner of a record 18 major championships, is the Golden Bear.

medium.question: Hitting your ball into a penalty area (water) costs a one-stroke penalty.
medium.explanation: True — you drop with a one-stroke penalty (or replay the shot).

medium.question: What does a golfer's handicap measure?
options: Their playing ability relative to par
The number of rounds played per year
medium.explanation: A handicap expresses scoring ability relative to par, letting players of different levels compete fairly.

medium.question: What is a "links" course?
options: A course connected to a hotel
A coastal course on sandy, dune-covered ground
medium.explanation: Links courses sit on sandy coastal land — firm turf, dunes, wind, and few trees. The oldest style of golf course.

medium.question: Which major championship is traditionally the first of the calendar year?
medium.explanation: The Masters opens the major season every April at Augusta National.

medium.question: In match play, how is the winner decided?
options: Fewest total strokes over the round
Best score on the final hole
medium.explanation: Match play is scored hole by hole — win more holes than your opponent and the total stroke count doesn't matter.

medium.question: What is the slightly longer grass bordering the green called?
medium.explanation: The fringe (or apron) rings the green — you can putt or chip from it.

medium.question: Complete the sentence:
medium.template: A hole completed in one stroke is called a hole-in-one, or an ___.
medium.explanation: A hole-in-one is also known as an ace.

hard.question: Which of these are men's major championships? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: The four majors are the Masters, PGA Championship, US Open and The Open. The Players, despite its strength, is not a major.

hard.question: Tiger Woods shares the record of 82 PGA Tour wins with which golfer?
hard.explanation: Sam Snead's 82 wins stood alone for decades until Tiger Woods equalled it in 2019.

hard.question: Which course is known as "the Home of Golf"?
options: The Old Course at St Andrews
hard.explanation: Golf has been played at St Andrews since the 15th century; the Old Course is the game's spiritual home.

hard.question: "Amen Corner" at Augusta National refers to which holes?
hard.explanation: Holes 11–13, where wind and water make and break Masters Sundays, were dubbed Amen Corner in 1958.

hard.question: The Claret Jug is the trophy awarded to the winner of which tournament?
hard.explanation: The Open champion lifts the Claret Jug — officially the Golf Champion Trophy, first presented in 1873.

hard.question: Who holds the record for most men's major championship wins?
hard.explanation: Jack Nicklaus won 18 majors between 1962 and 1986. Tiger Woods sits second with 15.

hard.question: The Solheim Cup is the women's equivalent of which event?
hard.explanation: The Solheim Cup pits the best women of the USA against Europe, mirroring the Ryder Cup format.

hard.question: A ball is out of bounds only when ALL of it lies beyond the boundary line.
hard.explanation: True — if any part of the ball touches or overhangs the course side of the line, it's still in play.

hard.question: In standard Stableford scoring, how many points does a birdie earn?
hard.explanation: Stableford awards 2 points for par, 3 for birdie, 4 for eagle, 1 for bogey.

hard.question: Name two of the four men's major championships.
hard.explanation: The four majors: the Masters, the PGA Championship, the US Open and The Open Championship.

elite.question: How many greens does the Old Course at St Andrews have in total?
elite.explanation: Seven huge double greens plus four single greens (1st, 9th, 17th, 18th) make 11 in total.

elite.question: Who won the original calendar-year Grand Slam in 1930?
elite.explanation: Amateur Bobby Jones won the US Open, The Open, US Amateur and British Amateur in 1930 — then retired at 28.

elite.question: What does a Stimpmeter measure?
elite.explanation: A Stimpmeter rolls a ball down a ramp onto the green; the distance it runs gives the green's speed in feet.

elite.question: What is a score of FOUR strokes under par on a single hole called?
elite.explanation: The condor — a hole-in-one on a par 5 — has been recorded only a handful of times, never in professional play.

elite.question: Which 20-year-old amateur famously won the 1913 US Open, popularising golf in America?
elite.explanation: Local caddie Francis Ouimet beat British stars Vardon and Ray in a playoff at Brookline — a watershed for American golf.

elite.question: The 1977 "Duel in the Sun" at Turnberry was fought between which two players?
elite.explanation: Watson edged Nicklaus by one stroke after they traded blows for two final rounds of 65-65 vs 65-66.

elite.question: Harry Vardon, after whom the Vardon grip is named, won The Open how many times?
elite.explanation: Vardon's six Open Championships (1896–1914) remain the record.

elite.question: Complete the sentence:
elite.template: In 1971, Apollo 14 astronaut Alan ___ hit golf balls on the surface of the Moon.
elite.explanation: Alan Shepard smuggled a modified 6-iron head aboard and hit two balls — the second going "miles and miles" in lunar gravity.

elite.question: What is the lowest single round ever shot in a men's major championship?
elite.explanation: Branden Grace shot 62 at Royal Birkdale in the 2017 Open, breaking the long-standing barrier of 63.

elite.question: Which player holds the record for most LPGA major championship wins?
elite.explanation: Patty Berg won 15 majors between 1937 and 1958 — still the record in women's golf.
